lines.text: Should I put down my employer or personal plan?

lines.speaker: Admin
lines.text: Look over section three — signature and date.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I filled out every field and looked over it twice.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: US forms are scary until you fill out, look over, and put down the right info.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Next: Follow-Up Visit

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: Thanks for coming back for the follow-up.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: Should I come back if symptoms return?

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: We'll look into the labs again if anything changes.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: The follow-up visit was shorter — I knew the vocabulary.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: You follow up well. That's half of healthcare in America.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Next: Health Clinic Finale

lines.speaker: Pharmacist
lines.text: Take it easy for a few days — hydration helps.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I'll look after myself better this sprint.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: You'll come around faster if you sleep eight hours.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: Health English: appointment, symptoms, forms, follow-up. I come around confident.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Look after yourself. Take it easy. You'll come around — always do.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Health Clinic Arc — Complete.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: You're taking on a side project after Big Tech hours?

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I'll put together an MVP in two weekends — go for it or regret it.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: If you take on too much, burnout comes back.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I'm taking on one small idea. I'll put together a landing page first.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Go for it. Put together something ugly that works.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Next: User Feedback Night

lines.speaker: User
lines.text: The onboarding confused me — I got lost on step two.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I'll take feedback openly. Let me go through your notes tonight.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Can you come up with three fixes before next beta?

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I went through every comment and came up with a simpler flow.

lines.speaker: User
lines.text: You take feedback well. Most founders get defensive.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Next: Launch Day Nerves

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: We're going live in ten minutes — my hands are shaking.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Look out for error spikes in the first hour.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: If the site goes down, you fix it. That's launch day.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: It went live! Nothing went down — yet. I'm looking out for every alert.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: You went live calm. You looked out for users. Launch day survived.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Next: Iterate After Launch

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Retention is soft — go back to onboarding and simplify.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I'll put together a patch release this weekend.

lines.speaker: User email
lines.text: Can you come up with dark mode?

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: We went back to basics, put together v1.1, came up with two quick wins.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Side projects live on iterate, iterate, iterate.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Next: Side Project Finale

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: Taking stock — one hundred users, zero revenue, infinite learning.

lines.speaker: Audience
lines.text: Your launch post went a long way — we saw it on HN.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Look forward to v2, but celebrate v1 tonight.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: Side project English: take on, feedback, go live, iterate.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Take stock, go a long way, look forward. Indie life suits you.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Side Project Arc — Complete.

lines.speaker: NYC
lines.text: I'll get back to you on the RFC when I'm online at 6 AM your time.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I'll follow up on the deployment thread before standup.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Async only works if everyone comes through on commitments.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I posted the update — no meeting needed. Follow up if blocked.

lines.speaker: NYC
lines.text: You came through on the doc. Async standup win.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Next: Written RFC Decision

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Put the decision in writing — no verbal-only agreements.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I'll go over every comment before we lock the RFC.

lines.speaker: Aoi
lines.text: Take note of the latency concern on page three.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: We put it in writing, went over objections, took note of every risk.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Written decisions scale. Meetings don't.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Next: Missed Handoff

lines.speaker: London
lines.text: Sorry — I forgot to hand off the pager properly.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I'll pick up the incident — what do I need to know?

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: When handoffs fail, someone must come through for the team.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I picked up at 5 AM, handled it, and fixed the handoff doc.

lines.speaker: London
lines.text: You came through for us. I'll never miss handoff again.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Next: Remote Async Finale

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: We go async on updates — meetings are for conflict only.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I'll take ownership of the handoff checklist going forward.

lines.speaker: Aoi
lines.text: Look ahead: document everything you'd say in a hallway chat.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: Remote English: follow up, put in writing, hand off, pick up.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Go async, take ownership, look ahead. You're remote-ready.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Remote Async Arc — Complete.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Quick drill — look into or take on a new service?

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: Look into the bug. Take on the migration project.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Look out for regressions. Take care of the on-call handoff.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: Look forward to ship day. Take stock after launch.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Look and take — nailed it. Put and come tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Next: Put & Come Mix-Up

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Don't put off what came up in standup.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: I'll come through on the fix and go through QA tonight.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Put forward the RFC, then go ahead after approval.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: Put, come, go — different prepositions, different worlds.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: One more episode. Grand finale next.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Next: Phrasal Review Grand Finale

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: Look into problems. Take ownership. Put ideas in writing.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: Come through for your team. Go the extra mile every day.

lines.speaker: Kenji
lines.text: Ninety-two episodes. Five verbs. One voice — yours.

lines.speaker: Audience
lines.text: You came full circle from shy newcomer to this stage.

lines.speaker: Nam
lines.text: Episodes 119 to 210 — look, take, put, come, go. It goes a long way. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Caption
lines.text: Phrasal Review Grand Finale — Episodes 119–210 Complete.
